title: How exactly does eva 00 self destruct anyway split
display_title: How exactly does eva 00 self destruct anyway split
wikitext:
[]: How exactly does eva 00 self destruct, anyway? [split]             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 29, 2014 12:04 pm [This thread split rather messily from this one , hence why it's not immediately about the title topic...] Bagheera wrote: I admit I'm unsure about the Angel tower, though; I think that might be the one place where you're absolutely correct about it being a tie-in to EoE, since I can't see any way it directly ties to other elements of the Broadcast version of the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Sep 29, 2014 12:07 pm Reichu wrote: How in the world does the Angel tower tie into EoE? (I'm going to assume by default that nobody actually believes my suggestion on that front...)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 29, 2014 12:26 pm Bagheera wrote: I was thinking more the GNR bit.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Sep 29, 2014 12:35 pm Reichu wrote: While it does clearly forecast EoE, that doesn't need to be its only purpose. Note that it's the Eva that transitions* into a giant Rei, which seems like a weird choice, unless this is a weird visual hint that Eva-00 and Rei are connected in the way oft speculated. The unification of Rei and Eva implied in the shot also suggests to some that their souls become one again. I'm not sure why sucking an Angel into one's core, making the core implode, and then blowing up would cause this, but whatever.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 29, 2014 12:54 pm Bagheera wrote: the explosion kills both Rei and the Eva, thus releasing the fragments of Lilith's soul.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Sep 29, 2014 1:04 pm Reichu wrote: Rei has an intact (albeit fried) corpse in the remains of the entry plug (this is what Ritsuko tells the demolition team to recover, I think), so whatever soul there is would be in there. A lot of weird stuff happened to Eva-00's core, and maybe that combined with whatever Armisael was doing (possibly more soul shenanigans) led up to the Reis coming together.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 29, 2014 1:44 pm Bagheera wrote: Unit 00 was certainly destroyed, though, so maybe the fragment just hopped into Rei then (or rather, was pushed; she was probably in the core at the time of the explosion).

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Sep 29, 2014 2:00 pm Reichu wrote: The front of the entry plug does go into the core to synch with it, yeah. (Not sure how it managed to survive both an imploding core and an exploding Eva...)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 29, 2014 2:22 pm Bagheera wrote: They do that by overclocking the S2 organ, which the Evas don't have.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Sep 29, 2014 2:33 pm Reichu wrote: In episode 21', they say "The S2 Engine and the detonator are being linked!", suggesting that there actually is a mechanism separate from the S2 that's responsible for the whole blowing-up thing. (I'm going to assume that the "detonator" was not a feature added by the Katsuragi Team, because that wouldn't make sense even for Eva .)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 29, 2014 2:43 pm Bagheera wrote: Sure. The act of linking them is presumably what causes the explosion. But it's clear the S2 is involved somehow, so an Eva can't do that. I have to wonder what the detonator actually is; not clear why the Seeds or Angels would have such a thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Sep 29, 2014 2:49 pm Reichu wrote: Evas may not have an S2, but they are supplied with all the energy Japan can provide via umbilical cable. Adam et al. have the innate ability to make use of the S2's raw power, and I'd guess that Evas make use of electricity the same way. With the self-destruction business, the "detonator" (whatever that is) would already be there (since Evas clearly do explode, no denying that), and the energy for the explosion is provided from the umbilical feed. Meaning they hypothetically could've stopped Rei from blowing up Tokyo-3 if they shut off the power outlet remotely or something, but, eh.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Sep 29, 2014 3:00 pm Reichu wrote: The front of the entry plug does go into the core to synch with it, yeah. (Not sure how it managed to survive both an imploding core and an exploding Eva...)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 29, 2014 3:14 pm UrsusArctos wrote: I'd guess that the Entry Plug was designed such that it would be ejected at the last moment before detonation, but failed to get to anything like a safe radius thanks to Armisael messing up Eva-00. It's a lousy explanation, but I can't think of any other reason why that thing would still be in one piece (albeit with Kentucky Fried Rei inside)

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Mon Sep 29, 2014 3:36 pm Bagheera wrote: The fact that there's an actual self-destruct system suggests that Nerv installed an explosive of some kind (an N2 maybe?). No idea why they'd do that, but here we are.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Sep 29, 2014 3:55 pm ^ It has been quite a bit of time since that time the Angels invaded Eva Units for the first time in Episode 13. Maybe after that they were like “Hey, this might be a security risk again in the future.” So they installed a self-destruct system in the Eva that the pilot would have to be conscious in order to activate. Unfortunately for Toji, he wasn’t all there when his Unit got infected. So he couldn’t initiate any self-destruct program.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Sep 29, 2014 4:33 pm And we do know it's a program, too-- when Rei pulls her fancy lever, we can see an indicator on the back of the seat switch to something that says Program D. I like the idea of sensitive tech with a self-destruct mechanism, personally. It's just a good idea to make sure it doesn't go off when you don't want it to.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Sep 29, 2014 8:57 pm Well, at least I tried to explain how KFR could land up in that Entry Plug. Anyway... With the Bardiel case, there's no way to know what equipment was installed on Eva-03 in the first place, especially since Bardiel "field-modified" it with the parasitic goop and the extensible arms. It may or may not have had an explosive device at the pilot's disposal. Toji was apparently incapacitated throughout the Bardiel battle, and when the goop invaded his entry plug pulling on the self-destruct mechanism is not a likely reaction. Nerv had no explosive bolts or remote detonation for Eva-03 either. They were able to attempt ejecting the Entry Plug (held back by slime), so it's quite possible they'd have tried to blow off Eva-03's limbs with explosive bolts, or detonate the Eva altogether if they could. Now that I think about it, it's probably just Eva-00 that was installed with such self-destruct devices. Perhaps Nerv was afraid of Eva-00 being infected and taken over early on, or perhaps it was an attempt to prevent a berserk Eva-00 from going all-out on Nerv personnel if it continued to run without power. And to make the darkly obvious thing obvious - if Rei was ordered to blow herself up and take out an Angel with her, she would obey, and that would make Eva-00, the flaky, troublesome prototype, the only Eva truly fit for a kamikaze attack should need be. That's it for now, I guess...I'll join back in the discussion if it's going on next week.

[]:             Quote Post by JonnyNova » Mon Sep 29, 2014 9:17 pm Haivng something like an N2 mine powerful enough to create the explosion of 00 seems kind of unlikely. Back when the JA was being unveiled Dr. Akagi tried to use that to deride the JA's capability in hand to hand combat. Had the Evas had a similar capability the JHCI would countered with that immediately in the conference on top of the other points. While probably not on the same level as an S2 engine, the Eva cores could have many if not all of the features as the cores that the Angels use. I think that NERV just didn't figure out how to draw power off the core. 01 did active quite a number of times on it's own and/or without power before taking Zeruel's core. It is also possible this limitation could have also been built into the designs by SEELE to prevent the Evas as becoming as powerful as the gods they were copied from. In either case, the "let's beat it with a hammer until it explodes" feature could have been retained though.

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Mon Sep 29, 2014 9:28 pm JonnyNova wrote: Haivng something like an N2 mine powerful enough to create the explosion of 00 seems kind of unlikely. Back when the JA was being unveiled Dr. Akagi tried to use that to deride the JA's capability in hand to hand combat. Had the Evas had a similar capability the JHCI would countered with that immediately in the conference on top of the other points.
